title: Biivren

{{Quote|Biivren is a minor industrial world in the Expansion Region controlled by the Empire.|Platt Okeefe, ''Smuggler's Handbook''|<ref name="Platt" />}}
'''Biivren''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Biivren sector/Legends|Biivren sector]]. An [[Factory world/Legends|industrial world]] controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], it was the site of a large [[Biivren's industrial sector|zone]] of [[Factory/Legends|factories]] and [[Refinery/Legends|refineries]] that gradually contaminated the planet's [[Water/Legends|water]] supply.

The [[Blood Razors]] [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] operated on Biivren and protected the industrial sector's inhabitants. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] retreated from Biivren, leaving the world to face a shortage of [[Foodstuff/Legends|drinking]] water. A group of [[free-trader]]s subsequently shipped in large amounts of potable water to sell it to the highest bidder.

==Description==
Biivren was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Platt" /> located in the [[Biivren system/Legends|Biivren system]], a part of the [[Biivren sector/Legends|Biivren sector]]<ref name="EAOC Biivren" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA Biivren" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC Biivren" />

==History==
Biivren was controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and served as a minor [[Factory world/Legends|industrial world]].<ref name="Platt" /> When Biivren was taken over by large corporations, the [[Blood Razors]] [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] was formed to assist the outcast inhabitants<ref name="CSWE Blood Razors">{{CSWECite|I|80|Blood Razors}}</ref> of the planet's [[Biivren's industrial sector|industrial sector]]. Biivren was mentioned in the ''[[Smuggler's Handbook/Legends|Smuggler's Handbook]]'', a [[Book/Legends|book]] [[Author/Legends|authored]] by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]].<ref name="Platt" />

Within six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="Journal2" /> a confrontation between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] that saw the [[Death/Legends|demise]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the fragmentation of the Empire,<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] on Biivren withdrew from the planet. In the process, several of Biivren's [[Factory/Legends|factories]] and chemical plants were scuttled,<ref name="Journal2" /> intentionally<ref name="CSWE Biivren">{{CSWECite|I|73|Biivren}}</ref> finalizing the contamination of the planet's supply of [[Water/Legends|water]].<ref name="Journal2" />

{{RPGseed}}
Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], a group of [[free-trader]]s learned of the situation on Biivren. The [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] hatched a plan to transport [[Foodstuff/Legends|drinking]] water to Biivren and sell it to the highest bidder. Locating a source of large amounts of potable water, the group shipped it past [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] present in the Biivren [[Star system/Legends|system]] and subsequently located sales contacts in [[Biivren starport]]. In the process, the free-traders encountered greedy government officials, competing merchants, and [[Crime lord/Legends|crimelords]] wishing to control the trade in water.<ref name="Journal2" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Despite frequent Imperial patrols, an entire society of vagrants, petty criminals and refugees has flourished among the greasy alleys which honeycomb Biivren's industrial sector.|Platt Okeefe, ''Smuggler's Handbook''|<ref name="Platt" />}}
Although Imperial forces on Biivren frequently carried out patrols in the planet's industrial sector, vagrants, petty criminals, and [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] made up an entire society that thrived there. The [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] gang known as the Blood Razors operated in Biivren's factory area,<ref name="Platt" /> chiefly trafficking [[Black market/Legends|black-market]] goods to the underground<ref name="CSWE Blood Razors" /> and redistributing materials [[Thief/Legends|stolen]] from Imperial forces.<ref name="Platt" /> The gang avoided being noticed by Imperial forces on the planet by covering up their activities with swoop chases.<ref name="CSWE Blood Razors" />

The Blood Razors frequently made forays outside of their territory in order to raid Imperial and corporate facilities, attack [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] belonging to the corporations, and annoy officials of the planet's [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]]. In addition, while Biivren's industrial sector was officially governed by Imperial and local corporate authorities, the Blood Razors were the source of real order and justice in the area, with the local inhabitants being under their protection and most of the intruders there being subjected to harassment.<ref name="Platt" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|The spaceport, business and residential quarters are surrounded by kilometers of automated factories, chemical plants and refineries, all of which are gradually contaminating the planet's water supply.|Platt Okeefe, ''Smuggler's Handbook''|<ref name="Platt" />}}
Outside the sprawling<ref name="Journal2" /> Biivren starport and the planet's business and residential districts, automated factories, chemical plants, and refineries stretched on for [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]]. Biivren's industrial sector was honeycombed by greasy alleys, and the planet also featured Imperial supply depots. All of Biivren's industrial facilities were gradually contaminating the planet's water supply.<ref name="Platt" />

==Behind the scenes==
Biivren was introduced in "[[Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant]]," a [[May]] [[1994]] article authored by [[Peter M. Schweighofer|Peter Schweighofer]] and published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 2|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 2]]. "Recon & Report" includes an adventure seed for use with the [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', and this article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Journal2" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Biivren system, and therefore the planet Biivren, in grid square P-9.<ref name="TEA Biivren" />
